technology: Telegram removed; A2A protocol is the communication layer with a human interface built/exposed on top
source: John's directive 2026-05-11 + architecture-decisions-john.md #9 (originally framed 'Investigating' on 2026-04-27, settled by John on 2026-05-11).
summary: Telegram is removed from the CTO architecture. A2A protocol becomes the communication layer between hemispheres AND between CTO and John. A human-facing interface is built or exposed on top of A2A in a subsequent phase.
rationale: Telegram has multiple operational limitations that conflict with CTO's design: (1) bot conflict when cloning — two CTO instances cannot share one bot, breaking the clone-test-replace upgrade cycle; (2) no programmatic bot creation via BotFather — human bottleneck on every new bot; (3) /approve elevated-exec cards do not work on mobile (OpenClaw bugs #23856, #51245, #48499); (4) proactive messaging blocked until user messages bot first — bootstrap friction; (5) third-party messaging service dependency contradicts the principle of full system control. A2A is the open standard already adopted as the hemisphere corpus callosum (CTO-DECISION-005). Building/exposing a human interface on top of A2A unifies the communication stack — one protocol for hemisphere-to-hemisphere AND CTO-to-John traffic.

removed: Telegram bot creation from human-required prerequisites
Telegram retry / fallback policy from AGENTS.md failure handling
Telegram daily digest pipeline from HEARTBEAT.md
Telegram-related env vars (TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN, TELEGRAM_USER_ID) from .env requirements
Telegram references in architecture diagrams and current-state documentation
added: A2A registry process publishing both hemispheres' Agent Cards (install-plan.md §5.4)
Decision-log entry as the primary install completion artifact (in lieu of Telegram message)
Install report written to /opt/cto/logs/install/*.log instead of sent via Telegram
deferred: Human-facing interface on top of A2A — implementation in a subsequent phase, not v1.0 install scope. Until built, John interacts with CTO via Claude Code Remote Control sessions and direct A2A calls.

evaluation.what_it_makes_obsolete: CTO-DECISION-003 (Telegram Bot as primary comms). The bot itself (@HusbandCTObot) can be revoked via @BotFather at John's convenience — it is no longer part of the CTO infrastructure.
evaluation.cost_of_switching: Cascade Telegram removal across active documentation. Add A2A registry process to install plan. Defer human-interface build to a separate phase.
evaluation.cost_of_staying: Continued bot-conflict-on-clone friction, mobile /approve breakage, third-party messaging dependency.

test_results.notes: Validated during the install execution per test-plan.md §1.6 (no Telegram artefacts in either config) and §3.3 (no Telegram code paths fire under load).

rollback.instructions: If a human-interface build proves harder than expected, an interim revert to Telegram is possible — re-introduce the Telegram channel block in openclaw.json with John's bot token. But the protocol-layer decision (A2A as comms) stands; Telegram revert would be a temporary surface only.
